items.description: Compute random baseline for UAD F1 interpretation
items.rationale: F1=0.725 is unanchored without knowing what random selection achieves. This is the most critical missing experiment.
items.action: Randomly select 51 pairs from top 500 features, compute precision/recall/F1 against Chanin labels. Repeat 100 times. Report mean + std.

items.description: Remove or demote DFDA from primary contributions
items.rationale: DFDA's 99.5% improvement is artifactual. Keeping it as a primary contribution damages credibility.
items.action: Remove DFDA from contributions list. Relegate to single paragraph in Future Work. Remove Table 4 and Figure 3 (DFDA results).

items.rationale: Section 4.5 labeled 'E5' with no E4 is a structural defect that confuses readers.
items.action: Renumber sequentially: E1 (UAD full), E2 (multi-seed), E3 (cross-layer), E4 (DFDA). Update all references.

items.rationale: Table numbering mismatch between outline and paper causes compilation issues and confusion.
items.action: Standardize: Table 1=Main UAD Results, Table 2=DFDA Detail (if retained), Table 3=Prior Work. Update all in-text references.

items.rationale: Figure 1 is referenced but does not exist. This is a submission blocker.
items.action: Generate figures/fig1.pdf as a flow diagram showing the six-step UAD pipeline using matplotlib or TikZ.

items.description: Remove 'robustness' claims and reframe as determinism
items.rationale: Multi-seed consistency is determinism on a fixed SAE, not robustness. Calling it robustness misleads reviewers.
items.action: Rename Section 4.3 to 'Multi-Seed Determinism' or 'Reproducibility Validation.' Remove all 'robustness' claims unless tested across SAE retraining or corpus change.

items.description: Scope 'Any SAE, any corpus' claim to actual validation
items.rationale: The paper has validated UAD on one SAE, one model, one layer, one concept domain. 'Any SAE, any corpus' is unsupported overgeneralization.
items.action: Change applicability in Table 3 to 'GPT-2 Small, first-letter features' or 'Any SAE with sufficient co-occurrence statistics (validated on GPT-2 Small).'

items.rationale: Without ablations, design choices (HAC, phi coefficient, 50 clusters) are unjustified.
items.action: Run ablations: (1) k-means vs HAC, (2) raw co-occurrence vs phi coefficient, (3) 25 vs 50 vs 100 clusters, (4) top 250 vs 500 vs 1000 features. Each ~10 seconds.

items.description: Implement bootstrap CI and permutation test
items.rationale: No statistical testing means F1=0.725 has no statistical grounding.
items.action: Bootstrap CI: resample 1000 examples with replacement 1000 times, recompute F1, report 95% CI. Permutation test: shuffle cluster assignments, compute F1 under null, report p-value.

items.rationale: Understanding the 22 false positives would strengthen the method and guide post-hoc filtering.
items.action: Analyze the 22 false positive pairs: report phi coefficients, marginal frequencies, semantic patterns. Categorize (topical co-occurrence, shared super-absorber, random).

items.rationale: Layer 4 (F1=0.432) falls below threshold. Presenting mean F1=0.561 masks this failure.
items.action: Report layer-wise results individually. State clearly that UAD performs well at layer 8 but falls below threshold at layer 4.

items.description: Fix weak citation for layer optimality claim
items.rationale: Elhage et al. (2022) is about superposition, not layer-wise hierarchy structure.
items.action: Find a citation specifically about layer-wise hierarchy in transformers, or soften the claim.

items.description: Strengthen novelty claim against 'Geometry of Concepts'
items.rationale: Co-occurrence clustering on phi matrices is not novel. The novelty is in applying it to absorption detection.
items.action: Explicitly contrast UAD's parent-child pair identification with 'Geometry of Concepts' functional lobe discovery. Show that spectral clustering (their method) achieves lower F1 on absorption detection (ablation).

items.description: Verify or explain identical phi values in DFDA table
items.rationale: Pairs 1-4 and 6 all have phi=0.812, which is suspicious.
items.action: Verify phi values in source data. If correct, add footnote explaining why multiple pairs share the same phi.

items.rationale: Internal validation language should not appear in the paper draft.
items.action: Replace with standard prose: 'We designated cross-layer as a partial pass and proceeded to full experiments.'

items.rationale: A skeptical reviewer will question whether 1000 samples provides adequate coverage.
items.action: Add one sentence explaining that 1000 samples x ~15 tokens = ~15,000 activation events per feature, sufficient for phi coefficient estimation.

items.description: Fix P3 purpose statement in Section 4.1
items.rationale: P3 appears without context explaining why cross-layer validation was tested.
items.action: Add purpose clause: 'To test whether UAD's detection signature generalizes across model layers, we evaluated layers 4, 8, and 10.'

items.description: Prominently label DFDA subsection as preliminary
items.rationale: The caveat is strong but buried in paragraph 3. Readers scanning may miss it.
items.action: Add '**Preliminary Result -- Metric Under Revision.**' at the start of Section 4.5. Move caveat to first paragraph.

summary: 5 critical fixes (random baseline, DFDA demotion, E4 numbering, table numbering, Figure 1, robustness reframing, scope claims), 6 major improvements (ablations, statistical testing, false positive analysis, honest cross-layer reporting, citation fix, novelty strengthening), 5 minor polish items.
